title: Nano Banana Protein Pancakes
description: Fluffy, delicious protein pancakes naturally sweetened with bananas and packed with protein for the perfect post-workout breakfast.
instructions: In a blender, combine the banana, protein powder, egg whites, oats, baking powder, and cinnamon. Blend until completely smooth.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium-low heat and lightly coat with cooking spray.
Pour batter to form pancakes of your desired size. Cook until bubbles form on the top surface.
Carefully flip and cook the other side until lightly golden brown. Repeat with remaining batter.
Stack them high, garnish with fresh berries if desired, and enjoy warm!

title: Gourmet Grilled Chicken & Quinoa Bowl
description: Tender, herb-marinated grilled chicken breast served over a fluffy bed of quinoa, roasted seasonal vegetables, and a light lemon-herb vinaigrette.
instructions: Marinate the chicken breast with lemon juice, half of the olive oil, and fresh minced herbs.
Grill the chicken breast and sliced vegetables over medium-high heat until cooked through and beautifully charred.
Allow the grilled chicken to rest for a couple of minutes, then slice into elegant strips.
Assemble your bowl by layering cooked quinoa at the bottom, then topping with the sliced grilled chicken and colorful vegetables.
Drizzle with the remaining olive oil and a squeeze of fresh lemon juice before serving.

title: High-Protein Avocado Toast with Poached Eggs
description: Whole-grain sourdough toast topped with creamy mashed avocado, baby spinach, and two perfectly poached eggs for a premium, clean-eating lunch.

ingredients.name: 2 thick slices of whole-grain sourdough bread
Salt and black pepper to taste

instructions: Toast the slices of sourdough bread to golden perfection.
In a small bowl, mash the avocado flesh with lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Poach the eggs in simmering water with a drop of vinegar for 3 to 4 minutes until the whites are set but yolks remain perfectly runny.
Spread the seasoned mashed avocado evenly over the toasted sourdough, top with baby spinach, and carefully place poached eggs on top.
Sprinkle with red pepper flakes and freshly ground black pepper.

title: Seared Sesame Crust Ahi Tuna Salad
description: A luxurious, protein-packed pan-seared sesame ahi tuna steak served over a bed of crisp greens, cucumber, and a light sesame-soy ginger dressing.

ingredients.name: 2 tbsp mixed white and black sesame seeds

instructions: Coat the Ahi tuna steak evenly with the mixed sesame seeds, pressing them gently to adhere.
Heat a non-stick pan with half the sesame oil over high heat.
Sear the tuna steak for 1-2 minutes on each side for a beautiful rare center.
Whisk together the soy sauce, remaining sesame oil, and ginger to create the dressing.
Toss the mixed greens and cucumber in the dressing, arrange on a plate, slice the tuna, and lay it over the salad.

title: Greek Yogurt Chia Seed Pudding
description: Thick Greek yogurt blended with chia seeds, pure vanilla, and honey, chilled overnight and topped with fresh berries and almonds.

ingredients.name: 1 tsp organic honey or maple syrup

instructions: In a jar or container, thoroughly mix the Greek yogurt, chia seeds, honey, and vanilla extract.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours (or overnight) to allow the chia seeds to swell and thicken.
Stir the pudding before serving to ensure a creamy consistency.
Garnish with fresh berries and sliced almonds for a satisfying, high-protein snack.

title: Lemon Herb Grilled Salmon with Asparagus
description: Premium wild-caught grilled salmon fillet seasoned with fresh lemon zest, garlic, and dill, paired with tender, charred asparagus spears.

ingredients.name: Sea salt and cracked black pepper

instructions: Preheat your grill or a stove-top grill pan to medium-high heat.
Pat salmon fillets dry and brush lightly with avocado oil, then rub with minced garlic, lemon zest, dill, salt, and pepper.
Toss the trimmed asparagus spears with the remaining avocado oil, salt, and pepper.
Place the salmon on the grill skin-side down first and cook for 4-5 minutes, then carefully flip and cook for an additional 3-4 minutes.
Grill the asparagus alongside the salmon for 5-7 minutes until tender and lightly charred.
Serve hot with lemon slices and fresh dill garnish.

title: Herb-Baked Turkey and Spinach Meatballs
description: Extra-lean ground turkey mixed with fresh finely chopped spinach, garlic, onion, and herbs, baked to juicy perfection.

ingredients.name: 1 cup fresh baby spinach, very finely chopped
Sea salt and black pepper to taste

instructions: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C) and line a large baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a large bowl, combine the ground turkey, chopped spinach, minced onion, garlic, almond flour, egg, Italian seasoning, salt, and pepper.
Mix gently with your hands until just combined (do not overwork the meat).
Roll the mixture into small, even-sized meatballs (makes about 12-15 meatballs) and arrange them on the baking sheet.
Bake for 18-20 minutes until cooked through and the tops are lightly browned.

title: Clean Beef and Broccoli Stir-Fry
description: High-protein lean flank steak strips flash-fried with fresh broccoli florets in a low-sodium ginger, garlic, and coconut aminos savory sauce.

ingredients.name: 300g lean flank steak, sliced into thin strips
1/4 cup coconut aminos or low-sodium soy sauce

instructions: In a small bowl, whisk coconut aminos, half the sesame oil, ginger, garlic, and cornstarch with 2 tbsp of water.
Heat a large wok or skillet over high heat with the remaining sesame oil.
Add the flank steak strips and cook for 3-4 minutes until nicely browned, then remove from the pan and set aside.
Add the broccoli florets and 2 tablespoons of water to the wok, covering to steam for 2-3 minutes until tender-crisp.
Return the steak to the wok, pour in the sauce mixture, and stir continuously for 1-2 minutes until the sauce bubbles and thickens.
Garnish with sesame seeds and serve warm.

title: Spicy Cilantro Lime Shrimp with Cauliflower Rice
description: Vibrant, garlic-sautéed jumbo shrimp tossed with fresh lime juice and spicy chili flakes, served over high-fiber riced cauliflower.
instructions: Heat half of the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the cauliflower rice and cook for 5-6 minutes until tender, then season lightly and transfer to serving bowls.
In the same skillet, add the remaining olive oil and sliced garlic. Sauté for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the shrimp to the pan and season with red pepper flakes, salt, and pepper. Cook for 2-3 minutes per side until pink and opaque.
Remove skillet from heat, stir in the fresh lime juice, lime zest, and chopped cilantro, tossing to coat the shrimp.
Spoon the spicy shrimp and flavorful pan juices over the cauliflower rice and serve immediately.

title: High-Protein Cottage Cheese Berry Bowl
description: Creamy low-fat cottage cheese whipped with a touch of honey, topped with an organic berry mix, sliced almonds, and a dusting of hemp seeds.

ingredients.name: 1/2 cup fresh mixed berries (blueberries, raspberries, blackberries)
1 tbsp organic raw honey or pure maple syrup

instructions: For an ultra-creamy texture, blend the cottage cheese in a food processor or blender for 1 minute until completely smooth (optional).
Spoon the cottage cheese into a wide breakfast bowl.
Arrange the fresh mixed berries and toasted sliced almonds beautifully over the top.
Drizzle with raw honey and sprinkle with hemp seeds and ground cinnamon.

title: Baked Sesame Tofu with Steamed Bok Choy
description: Crispy baked organic firm tofu cubes marinated in ginger-sesame soy sauce, paired with beautifully steamed fresh baby bok choy.

ingredients.name: 400g extra-firm organic tofu, pressed and cubed
4 baby bok choy heads, halved lengthwise

instructions: In a shallow dish, whisk together soy sauce, sesame oil, maple syrup, and grated ginger. Add tofu cubes, toss gently, and marinate for 15 minutes.
Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Remove tofu from marinade (reserve marinade), toss tofu cubes gently with cornstarch, and arrange on the baking sheet in a single layer.
Bake tofu for 25-30 minutes, turning halfway through, until crispy and golden.
Steam baby bok choy halves for 4-5 minutes until bright green and tender-crisp.
Drizzle remaining marinade over steamed bok choy, plate with the crispy tofu, and garnish with sesame seeds.

title: Mediterranean Herb Chicken Quinoa Salad
description: Juicy grilled Mediterranean-style chicken strips tossed with cooled organic quinoa, crisp cucumbers, cherry tomatoes, kalamata olives, and light feta cheese.

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup kalamata olives, pitted and halved
1 tbsp red wine vinegar or fresh lemon juice

instructions: Season chicken breasts with oregano, basil, salt, and pepper. Grill or pan-sear until fully cooked, then slice into strips.
In a large salad bowl, combine the cooled quinoa, cucumber, cherry tomatoes, kalamata olives, and crumbled feta cheese.
In a small cup, whisk together olive oil, red wine vinegar, salt, and pepper to create a dressing.
Add the sliced grilled chicken to the bowl and drizzle with the vinaigrette dressing.
Toss gently to integrate all ingredients and serve chilled or at room temperature.

title: Egg White Veggie Scramble with Turkey Bacon
description: A clean, high-protein egg white scramble loaded with fresh bell peppers, spinach, and mushrooms, served with crispy extra-lean turkey bacon.

ingredients.name: 1 tsp avocado oil or cooking spray

instructions: In a small non-stick pan over medium heat, cook turkey bacon slices for 3-4 minutes per side until perfectly crispy. Remove and set aside.
Lightly coat the same pan with avocado oil or cooking spray. Add diced bell peppers and sliced mushrooms, sautéing for 3 minutes until tender.
Add baby spinach and stir for 1 minute until wilted.
Lower heat to medium-low, pour in the liquid egg whites, and cook, stirring gently and continuously, until soft and fully scrambled.
Plate the scramble warm alongside the crispy turkey bacon, and garnish with fresh chives.

title: Ginger Teriyaki Glazed Cod with Brown Rice
description: Flaky, baked Atlantic cod loin lightly glazed with a home-style honey-teriyaki ginger sauce, paired with high-fiber steamed brown rice.

ingredients.name: 1 cup cooked organic brown jasmine rice

instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C) and grease a small baking dish with cooking spray.
In a small bowl, whisk together low-sodium teriyaki, honey, and fresh grated ginger.
Place cod fillets in the baking dish and brush generously with the teriyaki-ginger glaze.
Bake for 12-15 minutes until the fish flakes easily with a fork.
To serve, place a bed of warm brown rice on plates, top with the baked glazed cod, arrange steamed sugar snap peas on the side, and garnish with sliced green onion.

title: Creamy Protein Oat Bran Bowl
description: Warm, creamy organic oat bran cooked with unsweetened almond milk, stirred with premium vanilla plant-based protein, flaxseeds, and fresh organic strawberries.
instructions: In a small saucepan, bring the unsweetened almond milk and oat bran to a gentle boil over medium heat.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly until thick and creamy.
Remove saucepan from heat. Let cool slightly for 1 minute, then add the protein powder and ground flaxseed, stirring vigorously until completely smooth.
Spoon the warm oat bran into a serving bowl.
Garnish with sliced fresh strawberries and sprinkle with chia seeds before enjoying.
